name: Bouyei

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Bouyei (bouy1240).

notes: Tai-Kadai (Northern Tai) language of Guizhou, China, written in the official 1985 Latin orthography based on the Wangmo County dialect. The map covers the consonant scheme (b/d/g as unaspirated voiceless stops, mb/nd as implosives, ng/ny nasals, x/h/r as fricatives per the scheme) and the base vowel letters with the ee/oo digraphs for open-mid qualities. Crucially, in this Zhuang-style orthography the syllable-final letters l, z, c, x, s, h (and variants j, f, y) are pure tone markers, not segments; a position-blind symbol-to-symbol reading cannot distinguish these tone letters from onset consonants, so tone is left unencoded here and word-final tone letters will surface as spurious consonants, substantially raising the expected error floor. Genuine stop codas share the letters b/d/g with tones 7-8. Some vowel-digraph values vary across descriptions of the scheme.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Bouyei (bouy1240)
